etymology.description: semantic 阝(阝) (fù, "hill; mound") + phonetic 完(完) (wán, "complete") -> courtyard
etymology.interpretation: The character 院(yuàn, "courtyard; institution") is formed by the semantic component 阝(阝) (fù, "hill; mound"), suggesting an enclosed or defined space, combined with the phonetic component 完(完) (wán, "complete"), contributing the sound. The idea of a 'courtyard' as a defined, enclosed space aligns with the semantic implication of 阝(阝). In extended use, the character refers to institutions, which are also defined spaces pertaining to specific functions.

examples.chinese: 我们在医院对面吃饭(我們在醫院對面吃飯)。
examples.english: We are eating opposite the hospital.

examples.chinese: 学校的院子里有很多花(學校的院子裡有很多花)。
examples.pinyin: Xuéxiào de yuànzi lǐ yǒu hěn duō huā.
examples.english: There are many flowers in the school's courtyard.

examples.chinese: 这家研究院专注于环保技术(這家研究院專注於環保技術)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiā yánjiūyuàn zhuānzhù yú huánbǎo jìshù.
examples.english: This research institute focuses on environmental technology.
